taxonomy: Desmidorchis speciosa is classified within the Apocynaceae family, a group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. This family includes various species of flowering plants, shrubs, and trees found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Desmidorchis is a small genus of flowering plants, with Desmidorchis speciosa being one of its species. Further classification places Desmidorchis speciosa within the tribe Plumeriae, which includes plants with similar characteristics. The classification of Desmidorchis speciosa reflects its unique combination of morphological and anatomical features.
